snippets.description: Single shared trust-boundary predicate. Manager -> sql`true` no-op; rep -> OR(own, everyone, unowned, EXISTS grant). The ONLY place the rule is expressed.
snippets.content: export function visibilityCondition(userId, role): SQL { if (role === 'manager') return sql`true`; return or(eq(ownerId,userId), eq(visibility,'everyone'), isNull(ownerId), exists(select 1 from crm_lead_visibility_grants where leadId=crmLeads.id and userId=:userId)); }

snippets.description: All 7 rep-facing read surfaces wired with visibilityCondition. getTodayQueue/getNavCounts/getRemindersQueue are owner-scoped (no-op wiring, defensive). getNavCounts unassigned sub-count is SPEC-exempt (not scoped).
snippets.content: conditions.push(visibilityCondition(userId, role)) at each read fn; getLead folds it into WHERE -> null (404) for barred reads.

snippets.description: Write-through: create/update persist visibility + grants transactionally (update writes crm_lead_history 'visibility' row + replaces/clears grants). All 3 owner-change paths reset visibility='everyone' + delete grants in-transaction.
SPEC-exempt surfaces explicitly NOT wired, with code comments: Up-for-Grabs (unowned always visible) and listPipelineLeads (manager-only /team caller).
snippets.content: // DO NOT wire visibilityCondition (manager-only surface / unowned always visible).

snippets.description: Additive enum + junction table (cascade lead FK, set-null user FK, unique index) + crm_leads.visibility NOT NULL DEFAULT 'everyone' backfill. NO Better Auth tables in diff.
snippets.content: CREATE TYPE crm_lead_visibility; CREATE TABLE crm_lead_visibility_grants(...); ALTER TABLE crm_leads ADD COLUMN visibility ... DEFAULT 'everyone' NOT NULL;
